NKJV.1: “Most assuredly, I say to you, he who does not enter the sheepfold by the door, but climbs up some other way, the same is a thief and a robber.
NKJV.2: But he who enters by the door is the shepherd of the sheep.
NKJV.3: To him the doorkeeper opens, and the sheep hear his voice; and he calls his own sheep by  name and leads them out.
NKJV.4: And when he brings out his own sheep, he goes before them; and the sheep follow him, for they know his voice.
NKJV.5: Yet they will by no means follow a  stranger, but will flee from him, for they do not know the voice of strangers.”
NKJV.6: Jesus used this illustration, but they did not understand the things which He spoke to them.
NKJV.7: Then Jesus said to them again, “Most assuredly, I say to you, I am the door of the sheep.
NKJV.8: All who ever came before Me are thieves and robbers, but the sheep did not hear them.
NKJV.9: I am the door. If anyone enters by Me, he will be saved, and will go in and out and find pasture.
NKJV.10: The thief does not come except to steal, and to kill, and to destroy. I have come that they may have life, and that they may have it more abundantly.
NKJV.11: “I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd gives His life for the sheep.
NKJV.12: But a hireling, he who is not the shepherd, one who does not own the sheep, sees the wolf coming and leaves the sheep and flees; and the wolf catches the sheep and scatters them.
NKJV.13: The hireling flees because he is a hireling and does not care about the sheep.
NKJV.14: I am the good shepherd; and I know My sheep, and am known by My own.
NKJV.15: As the Father knows Me, even so I know the Father;  and I lay down My life for the sheep.
NKJV.16: And other sheep I have which are not of this fold; them also I must bring, and they will hear My voice; and there will be one flock and one shepherd.
NKJV.17: “Therefore My Father  loves Me,  because I lay down My life that I may take it again.
NKJV.18: No one takes it from Me, but I lay it down of Myself. I  have power to lay it down, and I have power to take it again.  This command I have received from My Father.”
NKJV.19: Therefore there was a division again among the Jews because of these sayings.
NKJV.20: And many of them said, “He has a demon and is mad. Why do you listen to Him?”
NKJV.21: Others said, “These are not the words of one who has a demon. Can a demon open the eyes of the blind?”
NKJV.22: Now it was the Feast of Dedication in Jerusalem, and it was winter.
NKJV.23: And Jesus walked in the temple, in Solomon’s porch.
NKJV.24: Then the Jews surrounded Him and said to Him, “How long do You keep us in doubt? If You are the Christ, tell us plainly.”
NKJV.25: Jesus answered them, “I told you, and you do not believe.  The works that I do in My Father’s name, they  bear witness of Me.
NKJV.26: But  you do not believe, because you are not of My sheep, as I said to you.
NKJV.27: My sheep hear My voice, and I know them, and they follow Me.
NKJV.28: And I give them eternal life, and they shall never perish; neither shall anyone snatch them out of My hand.
NKJV.29: My Father, who has given them to Me, is greater than all; and no one is able to snatch them out of My Father’s hand.
NKJV.31: Then the Jews took up stones again to stone Him.
NKJV.32: Jesus answered them, “Many good works I have shown you from My Father. For which of those works do you stone Me?”
NKJV.33: The Jews answered Him, saying, “For a good work we do not stone You, but for blasphemy, and because You, being a Man, make Yourself God.”
NKJV.34: Jesus answered them, “Is it not written in your law,  ‘I said, “You are gods” ’?
NKJV.35: If He called them gods,  to whom the word of God came (and the Scripture  cannot be broken),
NKJV.36: do you say of Him  whom the Father sanctified and  sent into the world, ‘You are blaspheming,’  because I said, ‘I am  the Son of God’?
NKJV.37: If I do not do the works of My Father, do not believe Me;
NKJV.38: but if I do, though you do not believe Me, believe the works, that you may know and believe that the Father is in Me, and I in Him.”
NKJV.39: Therefore they sought again to seize Him, but He escaped out of their hand.
NKJV.40: And He went away again beyond the Jordan to the place where John was baptizing at first, and there He stayed.
NKJV.41: Then many came to Him and said, “John performed no sign, but all the things that John spoke about this Man were true.”
NKJV.42: And many believed in Him there.

ESV.1: “Truly, truly, I say to you, he who does not enter the sheepfold by the door but climbs in by another way, that man is a thief and a robber.
ESV.2: But he who enters by the door is the shepherd of the sheep.
ESV.3: To him the gatekeeper opens. The sheep hear his voice, and he calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.
ESV.4: When he has brought out all his own, he goes before them, and the sheep follow him, for they know his voice.
ESV.5: A stranger they will not follow, but they will flee from him, for they do not know the voice of strangers.”
ESV.6: This figure of speech Jesus used with them, but they did not understand what he was saying to them.
ESV.7: So Jesus again said to them, “Truly, truly, I say to you, I am the door of the sheep.
ESV.8: All who came before me are thieves and robbers, but the sheep did not listen to them.
ESV.9: I am the door. If anyone enters by me, he will be saved and will go in and out and find pasture.
ESV.10: The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy. I came that they may have life and have it abundantly.
ESV.11: I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
ESV.12: He who is a hired hand and not a shepherd, who does not own the sheep, sees the wolf coming and leaves the sheep and flees, and the wolf snatches them and scatters them.
ESV.13: He flees because he is a hired hand and cares nothing for the sheep.
ESV.14: I am the good shepherd. I know my own and my own know me,
ESV.15: just as the Father knows me and I know the Father; and I lay down my life for the sheep.
ESV.16: And I have other sheep that are not of this fold. I must bring them also, and they will listen to my voice. So there will be one flock, one shepherd.
ESV.17: For this reason the Father loves me, because I lay down my life that I may take it up again.
ESV.18: No one takes it from me, but I lay it down of my own accord. I have authority to lay it down, and I have authority to take it up again. This charge I have received from my Father.”
ESV.19: There was again a division among the Jews because of these words.
ESV.20: Many of them said, “He has a demon, and is insane; why listen to him?”
ESV.21: Others said, “These are not the words of one who is oppressed by a demon. Can a demon open the eyes of the blind?”
ESV.22: At that time the Feast of Dedication took place at Jerusalem. It was winter,
ESV.23: and Jesus was walking in the temple, in the colonnade of Solomon.
ESV.24: So the Jews gathered around him and said to him, “How long will you keep us in suspense? If you are the Christ, tell us plainly.”
ESV.25: Jesus answered them, “I told you, and you do not believe. The works that I do in my Father's name bear witness about me,
ESV.26: but you do not believe because you are not among my sheep.
ESV.27: My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me.
ESV.28: I give them eternal life, and they will never perish, and no one will snatch them out of my hand.
ESV.29: My Father, who has given them to me,  is greater than all, and no one is able to snatch them out of the Father's hand.
ESV.30: I and the Father are one.”
ESV.31: The Jews picked up stones again to stone him.
ESV.32: Jesus answered them, “I have shown you many good works from the Father; for which of them are you going to stone me?”
ESV.33: The Jews answered him, “It is not for a good work that we are going to stone you but for blasphemy, because you, being a man, make yourself God.”
ESV.34: Jesus answered them, “Is it not written in your Law, ‘I said, you are gods’?
ESV.35: If he called them gods to whom the word of God came — and Scripture cannot be broken —
ESV.36: do you say of him whom the Father consecrated and sent into the world, ‘You are blaspheming,’ because I said, ‘I am the Son of God’?

ESV.42: And many believed in him there.

NLT.1: “I tell you the truth, anyone who sneaks over the wall of a sheepfold, rather than going through the gate, must surely be a thief and a robber!
NLT.2: But the one who enters through the gate is the shepherd of the sheep.
NLT.3: The gatekeeper opens the gate for him, and the sheep recognize his voice and come to him. He calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.
NLT.4: After he has gathered his own flock, he walks ahead of them, and they follow him because they know his voice.
NLT.5: They won’t follow a stranger; they will run from him because they don’t know his voice.”
NLT.6: Those who heard Jesus use this illustration didn’t understand what he meant,
NLT.7: so he explained it to them: “I tell you the truth, I am the gate for the sheep.
NLT.8: All who came before me were thieves and robbers. But the true sheep did not listen to them.
NLT.9: Yes, I am the gate. Those who come in through me will be saved. They will come and go freely and will find good pastures.
NLT.10: The thief’s purpose is to steal and kill and destroy. My purpose is to give them a rich and satisfying life.
NLT.11: “I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd sacrifices his life for the sheep.
NLT.12: A hired hand will run when he sees a wolf coming. He will abandon the sheep because they don’t belong to him and he isn’t their shepherd. And so the wolf attacks them and scatters the flock.
NLT.13: The hired hand runs away because he’s working only for the money and doesn’t really care about the sheep.
NLT.14: “I am the good shepherd; I know my own sheep, and they know me,
NLT.15: just as my Father knows me and I know the Father. So I sacrifice my life for the sheep.
NLT.16: I have other sheep, too, that are not in this sheepfold. I must bring them also. They will listen to my voice, and there will be one flock with one shepherd.
NLT.17: “The Father loves me because I sacrifice my life so I may take it back again.
NLT.18: No one can take my life from me. I sacrifice it voluntarily. For I have the authority to lay it down when I want to and also to take it up again. For this is what my Father has commanded.”
NLT.19: When he said these things, the people were again divided in their opinions about him.
NLT.20: Some said, “He’s demon possessed and out of his mind. Why listen to a man like that?”
NLT.21: Others said, “This doesn’t sound like a man possessed by a demon! Can a demon open the eyes of the blind?”
NLT.22: It was now winter, and Jesus was in Jerusalem at the time of Hanukkah, the Festival of Dedication.
NLT.23: He was in the Temple, walking through the section known as Solomon’s Colonnade.
NLT.24: The people surrounded him and asked, “How long are you going to keep us in suspense? If you are the Messiah, tell us plainly.”
NLT.25: Jesus replied, “I have already told you, and you don’t believe me. The proof is the work I do in my Father’s name.
NLT.26: But you don’t believe me because you are not my sheep.
NLT.27: My sheep listen to my voice; I know them, and they follow me.
NLT.28: I give them eternal life, and they will never perish. No one can snatch them away from me,
NLT.29: for my Father has given them to me, and he is more powerful than anyone else. No one can snatch them from the Father’s hand.
NLT.30: The Father and I are one.”
NLT.31: Once again the people picked up stones to kill him.
NLT.32: Jesus said, “At my Father’s direction I have done many good works. For which one are you going to stone me?”
NLT.33: They replied, “We’re stoning you not for any good work, but for blasphemy! You, a mere man, claim to be God.”
NLT.34: Jesus replied, “It is written in your own Scriptures that God said to certain leaders of the people, ‘I say, you are gods!’
NLT.35: And you know that the Scriptures cannot be altered. So if those people who received God’s message were called ‘gods,’
NLT.36: why do you call it blasphemy when I say, ‘I am the Son of God’? After all, the Father set me apart and sent me into the world.
NLT.37: Don’t believe me unless I carry out my Father’s work.
NLT.38: But if I do his work, believe in the evidence of the miraculous works I have done, even if you don’t believe me. Then you will know and understand that the Father is in me, and I am in the Father.”
NLT.39: Once again they tried to arrest him, but he got away and left them.
NLT.40: He went beyond the Jordan River near the place where John was first baptizing and stayed there awhile.
NLT.41: And many followed him. “John didn’t perform miraculous signs,” they remarked to one another, “but everything he said about this man has come true.”
NLT.42: And many who were there believed in Jesus.

WEB.1: “Most certainly, I tell you, one who doesn’t enter by the door into the sheep fold, but climbs up some other way, the same is a thief and a robber.
WEB.2: But one who enters in by the door is the shepherd of the sheep.
WEB.3: The gatekeeper opens the gate for him, and the sheep listen to his voice. He calls his own sheep by name, and leads them out.
WEB.4: Whenever he brings out his own sheep, he goes before them, and the sheep follow him, for they know his voice.
WEB.5: They will by no means follow a stranger, but will flee from him; for they don’t know the voice of strangers.”
WEB.6: Jesus spoke this parable to them, but they didn’t understand what he was telling them.
WEB.7: Jesus therefore said to them again, “Most certainly, I tell you, I am the sheep’s door.
WEB.8: All who came before me are thieves and robbers, but the sheep didn’t listen to them.
WEB.9: I am the door. If anyone enters in by me, he will be saved, and will go in and go out, and will find pasture.
WEB.10: The thief only comes to steal, kill, and destroy. I came that they may have life, and may have it abundantly.
WEB.11: I am the good shepherd.  The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
WEB.12: He who is a hired hand, and not a shepherd, who doesn’t own the sheep, sees the wolf coming, leaves the sheep, and flees. The wolf snatches the sheep, and scatters them.
WEB.13: The hired hand flees because he is a hired hand, and doesn’t care for the sheep.
WEB.14: I am the good shepherd. I know my own, and I’m known by my own;
WEB.15: even as the Father knows me, and I know the Father. I lay down my life for the sheep.
WEB.16: I have other sheep, which are not of this fold.  I must bring them also, and they will hear my voice. They will become one flock with one shepherd.
WEB.17: Therefore the Father loves me, because I lay down my life,   that I may take it again.
WEB.18: No one takes it away from me, but I lay it down by myself. I have power to lay it down, and I have power to take it again. I received this commandment from my Father.”
WEB.19: Therefore a division arose again among the Jews because of these words.
WEB.20: Many of them said, “He has a demon, and is insane! Why do you listen to him?”
WEB.21: Others said, “These are not the sayings of one possessed by a demon. It isn’t possible for a demon to open the eyes of the blind, is it?”
WEB.22: It was the Feast of the Dedication  at Jerusalem.
WEB.23: It was winter, and Jesus was walking in the temple, in Solomon’s porch.
WEB.24: The Jews therefore came around him and said to him, “How long will you hold us in suspense? If you are the Christ, tell us plainly.”
WEB.25: Jesus answered them, “I told you, and you don’t believe. The works that I do in my Father’s name, these testify about me.
WEB.26: But you don’t believe, because you are not of my sheep, as I told you.
WEB.27: My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me.
WEB.28: I give eternal life to them. They will never perish, and no one will snatch them out of my hand.
WEB.29: My Father, who has given them to me, is greater than all. No one is able to snatch them out of my Father’s hand.
WEB.30: I and the Father are one.”
WEB.31: Therefore Jews took up stones again to stone him.
WEB.32: Jesus answered them, “I have shown you many good works from my Father. For which of those works do you stone me?”
WEB.33: The Jews answered him, “We don’t stone you for a good work, but for blasphemy: because you, being a man, make yourself God.”
WEB.34: Jesus answered them, “Isn’t it written in your law, ‘I said, you are gods?’
WEB.35: If he called them gods, to whom the word of God came (and the Scripture can’t be broken),
WEB.36: do you say of him whom the Father sanctified and sent into the world, ‘You blaspheme,’ because I said, ‘I am the Son of God?’
WEB.37: If I don’t do the works of my Father, don’t believe me.
WEB.38: But if I do them, though you don’t believe me, believe the works; that you may know and believe that the Father is in me, and I in the Father.”
WEB.39: They sought again to seize him, and he went out of their hand.
WEB.40: He went away again beyond the Jordan into the place where John was baptizing at first, and there he stayed.
WEB.41: Many came to him. They said, “John indeed did no sign, but everything that John said about this man is true.”

CSB17.20: Many of them were saying, “He has a demon <sup>ⓔ</sup> and he’s crazy. Why do you listen to him?”
CSB17.21: Others were saying, “These aren’t the words of someone who is demon-possessed. Can a demon open the eyes of the blind?” <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.22: Then the Festival of Dedication took place in Jerusalem, and it was winter.

CSB17.24: The Jews surrounded him and asked, “How long are you going to keep us in suspense? <sup>[3]</sup> If you are the Messiah, <sup>ⓗ</sup> tell us plainly.” <sup>[4]</sup> <sup>ⓘ</sup>

AMP.1: "I assure you and most solemnly say to you, he who does not enter by the door into the sheepfold, but climbs up from some other place [on the stone wall], that one is a thief and a robber.
AMP.2: But he who enters by the door is the shepherd of the sheep [the protector and provider].
AMP.3: The doorkeeper opens [the gate] for this man, and the sheep hear his voice and pay attention to it. And [knowing that they listen] he calls his own sheep by name and leads them out [to pasture].
AMP.4: When he has brought all his own sheep outside, he walks on ahead of them, and the sheep follow him because they know his voice and recognize his call.
AMP.5: They will never follow a stranger, but will run away from him, because they do not know the voice of strangers."
AMP.6: Jesus used this figure of speech with them, but they did not understand what He was talking about.
AMP.7: So Jesus said again, "I assure you and most solemnly say to you, I am the Door for the sheep [leading to life].
AMP.8: All who came before Me [as false messiahs and self-appointed leaders] are thieves and robbers, but the [true] sheep did not hear them.
AMP.9: I am the Door; anyone who enters through Me will be saved [and will live forever], and will go in and out [freely], and find pasture (spiritual security).
AMP.10: The thief comes only in order to steal and kill and destroy. I came that they may have and enjoy life, and have it in abundance [to the full, till it overflows].
AMP.11: I am the Good Shepherd. The Good Shepherd lays down His [own] life for the sheep.
AMP.12: But the hired man [who merely serves for wages], who is neither the shepherd nor the owner of the sheep, when he sees the wolf coming, deserts the flock and runs away; and the wolf snatches the sheep and scatters them.
AMP.13: The man runs because he is a hired hand [who serves only for wages] and is not concerned about the [safety of the] sheep.
AMP.14: I am the Good Shepherd, and I know [without any doubt those who are] My own and My own know Me [and have a deep, personal relationship with Me]—
AMP.15: even as the Father knows Me and I know the Father—and I lay down My [very own] life [sacrificing it] for the benefit of the sheep.
AMP.16: I have other sheep [beside these] that are not of this fold. I must bring those also, and they will listen to My voice and pay attention to My call, and they will become one flock with one Shepherd.
AMP.17: For this reason the Father loves Me, because I lay down My [own] life so that I may take it back.
AMP.18: No one takes it away from Me, but I lay it down voluntarily. I am authorized and have power to lay it down and to give it up, and I am authorized and have power to take it back. This command I have received from My Father."
AMP.19: A division [of opinion] occurred again among the Jews because of these words [of His].
AMP.20: Many of them said, "He has a demon and He is mad [insane—He raves and rambles]. Why listen to Him?"
AMP.21: Others were saying, "These are not the words and thoughts of one possessed by a demon. Can a demon open the eyes of the blind?"
AMP.22: At that time the Feast of Dedication took place at Jerusalem.
AMP.23: It was winter, and Jesus was walking in the temple [area] in Solomon's portico.
AMP.24: So the Jews surrounded Him and began saying to Him, "How long are You going to keep us in suspense? If You are [really] the Christ (the Messiah, the Anointed), tell us so plainly and openly."
AMP.25: Jesus answered them, "I have told you so, yet you do not believe. The works that I do in My Father's name testify concerning Me [they are My credentials and the evidence declaring who I am].
AMP.26: But you do not believe Me [so you do not trust and follow Me] because you are not My sheep.
AMP.27: The sheep that are My own hear My voice and listen to Me; I know them, and they follow Me.
AMP.28: And I give them eternal life, and they will never, ever [by any means] perish; and no one will ever snatch them out of My hand.
AMP.29: My Father, who has given them to Me, is greater and mightier than all; and no one is able to snatch them out of the Father's hand.
AMP.30: I and the Father are One [in essence and nature]."
AMP.31: Again the Jews picked up stones to stone Him.
AMP.32: Jesus answered them, "I showed you many good works [and many acts of mercy] from the Father; for which of them are you stoning Me?"
AMP.33: The Jews answered Him, "We are not going to stone You for a good work, but for blasphemy, because You, a mere man, make Yourself out to be God."
AMP.34: Jesus answered them, "Is it not written in your Law, 'I said, you are gods [human judges representing God, not divine beings]'?
AMP.35: If He called them gods, men to whom the word of God came (and the Scripture cannot be undone or annulled or broken),
AMP.36: [if that is true] then do you say of Him whom the Father sanctified and set apart for Himself and sent into the world, 'You are blaspheming,' because I said, 'I am the Son of God'?
AMP.37: If I do not do the works of My Father [that is, the miracles that only God could perform], then do not believe Me.
AMP.38: But if I am doing them, even if you do not believe Me or have faith in Me, [at least] believe the works [that I do—admit that they are the works of God], so that you may know and keep on knowing [clearly—without any doubt] that the Father is in Me, and I am in the Father [that is, I am One with Him]."
AMP.39: So they tried again to seize Him, but He eluded their grasp.
AMP.40: He went back again across the Jordan to the place where John was first baptizing, and He was staying there.
AMP.41: Many came to Him, and they were saying, "John did not perform a single sign (attesting miracle), but everything John said about this Man was true and accurate."
AMP.42: And many there believed and confidently trusted in Him [accepting Him as Savior, and following His teaching].

MSG.1: "Let me set this before you as plainly as I can. If a person climbs over or through the fence of a sheep pen instead of going through the gate, you know he's up to no good—a sheep rustler!
MSG.2: The shepherd walks right up to the gate.
MSG.3: The gatekeeper opens the gate to him and the sheep recognize his voice. He calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.
MSG.4: When he gets them all out, he leads them and they follow because they are familiar with his voice.
MSG.5: They won't follow a stranger's voice but will scatter because they aren't used to the sound of it."
MSG.6: Jesus told this simple story, but they had no idea what he was talking about.
MSG.7: So he tried again. "I'll be explicit, then. I am the Gate for the sheep.
MSG.8: All those others are up to no good—sheep stealers, every one of them. But the sheep didn't listen to them.
MSG.9: I am the Gate. Anyone who goes through me will be cared for—will freely go in and out, and find pasture.
MSG.10: A thief is only there to steal and kill and destroy. I came so they can have real and eternal life, more and better life than they ever dreamed of.
MSG.11: "I am the Good Shepherd. The Good Shepherd puts the sheep before himself, sacrifices himself if necessary.
MSG.12: A hired man is not a real shepherd. The sheep mean nothing to him. He sees a wolf come and runs for it, leaving the sheep to be ravaged and scattered by the wolf.
MSG.13: He's only in it for the money. The sheep don't matter to him.
MSG.14: "I am the Good Shepherd. I know my own sheep and my own sheep know me.
MSG.15: In the same way, the Father knows me and I know the Father. I put the sheep before myself, sacrificing myself if necessary.
MSG.16: You need to know that I have other sheep in addition to those in this pen. I need to gather and bring them, too. They'll also recognize my voice. Then it will be one flock, one Shepherd.
MSG.17: This is why the Father loves me: because I freely lay down my life. And so I am free to take it up again.
MSG.18: No one takes it from me. I lay it down of my own free will. I have the right to lay it down; I also have the right to take it up again. I received this authority personally from my Father."
MSG.19: This kind of talk caused another split in the Jewish ranks.
MSG.20: A lot of them were saying, "He's crazy, a maniac—out of his head completely. Why bother listening to him?"
MSG.21: But others weren't so sure: "These aren't the words of a crazy man. Can a 'maniac' open blind eyes?"
MSG.22: They were celebrating Hanukkah just then in Jerusalem. It was winter.
MSG.23: Jesus was strolling in the Temple across Solomon's Porch.
MSG.24: The Jews, circling him, said, "How long are you going to keep us guessing? If you're the Messiah, tell us straight out."
MSG.25: Jesus answered, "I told you, but you don't believe. Everything I have done has been authorized by my Father, actions that speak louder than words.
MSG.26: You don't believe because you're not my sheep.
MSG.27: My sheep recognize my voice. I know them, and they follow me.
MSG.28: I give them real and eternal life. They are protected from the Destroyer for good. No one can steal them from out of my hand.
MSG.29: The Father who put them under my care is so much greater than the Destroyer and Thief. No one could ever get them away from him.
MSG.30: I and the Father are one heart and mind."
MSG.31: Again the Jews picked up rocks to throw at him.
MSG.32: Jesus said, "I have made a present to you from the Father of a great many good actions. For which of these acts do you stone me?"
MSG.33: The Jews said, "We're not stoning you for anything good you did, but for what you said—this blasphemy of calling yourself God."
MSG.34: Jesus said, "I'm only quoting your inspired Scriptures, where God said, 'I tell you—you are gods.'
MSG.35: If God called your ancestors 'gods'—and Scripture doesn't lie—
MSG.36: why do you yell, 'Blasphemer! Blasphemer!' at the unique One the Father consecrated and sent into the world, just because I said, 'I am the Son of God'?
MSG.37: If I don't do the things my Father does, well and good; don't believe me.
MSG.38: But if I am doing them, put aside for a moment what you hear me say about myself and just take the evidence of the actions that are right before your eyes. Then perhaps things will come together for you, and you'll see that not only are we doing the same thing, we are the same—Father and Son. He is in me; I am in him."
MSG.39: They tried yet again to arrest him, but he slipped through their fingers.
MSG.40: He went back across the Jordan to the place where John first baptized, and stayed there.
MSG.41: A lot of people followed him over. They were saying, "John did no miracles, but everything he said about this man has come true."
MSG.42: Many believed in him then and there.

NIV.1:
The Shepherd and His Flock
“I tell you the truth, the man who does not enter the sheep pen by the gate, but climbs in by some other way, is a thief and a robber.
NIV.2: The man who enters by the gate is the shepherd of his sheep.
NIV.3: The watchman opens the gate for him, and the sheep listen to his voice. He calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.
NIV.4: When he has brought out all his own, he goes on ahead of them, and his sheep follow him because they know his voice.
NIV.5: But they will never follow a stranger; in fact, they will run away from him because they do not recognize a stranger’s voice.”
NIV.6: Jesus used this figure of speech, but they did not understand what he was telling them.
NIV.7: Therefore Jesus said again, “I tell you the truth, I am the gate for the sheep.
NIV.8: All who ever came before me were thieves and robbers, but the sheep did not listen to them.
NIV.9: I am the gate; whoever enters through me will be saved. He will come in and go out, and find pasture.
NIV.10: The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy; I have come that they may have life, and have it to the full.
NIV.11: “I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
NIV.12: The hired hand is not the shepherd who owns the sheep. So when he sees the wolf coming, he abandons the sheep and runs away. Then the wolf attacks the flock and scatters it.
NIV.13: The man runs away because he is a hired hand and cares nothing for the sheep.
NIV.14: “I am the good shepherd; I know my sheep and my sheep know me—
NIV.15: just as the Father knows me and I know the Father—and I lay down my life for the sheep.
NIV.16: I have other sheep that are not of this sheep pen. I must bring them also. They too will listen to my voice, and there shall be one flock and one shepherd.
NIV.17: The reason my Father loves me is that I lay down my life—only to take it up again.
NIV.18: No one takes it from me, but I lay it down of my own accord. I have authority to lay it down and authority to take it up again. This command I received from my Father.”
NIV.19: At these words the Jews were again divided.
NIV.20: Many of them said, “He is demon-possessed and raving mad. Why listen to him?”
NIV.21: But others said, “These are not the sayings of a man possessed by a demon. Can a demon open the eyes of the blind?”
NIV.22:
The Unbelief of the Jews
Then came the Feast of Dedication at Jerusalem. It was winter,
NIV.23: and Jesus was in the temple area walking in Solomon’s Colonnade.
NIV.24: The Jews gathered around him, saying, “How long will you keep us in suspense? If you are the Christ, tell us plainly.”
NIV.25: Jesus answered, “I did tell you, but you do not believe. The miracles I do in my Father’s name speak for me,
NIV.26: but you do not believe because you are not my sheep.
NIV.27: My sheep listen to my voice; I know them, and they follow me.
NIV.28: I give them eternal life, and they shall never perish; no one can snatch them out of my hand.
NIV.29: My Father, who has given them to me, is greater than all; no one can snatch them out of my Father’s hand.
NIV.30: I and the Father are one.”
NIV.31: Again the Jews picked up stones to stone him,
NIV.32: but Jesus said to them, “I have shown you many great miracles from the Father. For which of these do you stone me?”
NIV.33: “We are not stoning you for any of these,” replied the Jews, “but for blasphemy, because you, a mere man, claim to be God.”
NIV.34: Jesus answered them, “Is it not written in your Law, ‘I have said you are gods’?
NIV.35: If he called them ‘gods,’ to whom the word of God came—and the Scripture cannot be broken—
NIV.36: what about the one whom the Father set apart as his very own and sent into the world? Why then do you accuse me of blasphemy because I said, ‘I am God’s Son’?
NIV.37: Do not believe me unless I do what my Father does.
NIV.38: But if I do it, even though you do not believe me, believe the miracles, that you may know and understand that the Father is in me, and I in the Father.”
NIV.39: Again they tried to seize him, but he escaped their grasp.
NIV.40: Then Jesus went back across the Jordan to the place where John had been baptizing in the early days. Here he stayed
NIV.41: and many people came to him. They said, “Though John never performed a miraculous sign, all that John said about this man was true.”
NIV.42: And in that place many believed in Jesus.

NET.1: “I tell you the solemn truth, the one who does not enter the sheepfold by the door, but climbs in some other way, is a thief and a robber.
NET.2: The one who enters by the door is the shepherd of the sheep.
NET.3: The doorkeeper opens the door for him, and the sheep hear his voice. He calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.
NET.4: When he has brought all his own sheep out, he goes ahead of them, and the sheep follow him because they recognize his voice.
NET.5: They will never follow a stranger, but will run away from him, because they do not recognize the stranger’s voice.”
NET.6: Jesus told them this parable, but they did not understand what he was saying to them.
NET.7: So Jesus said to them again, “I tell you the solemn truth, I am the door for the sheep.
NET.8: All who came before me were thieves and robbers, but the sheep did not listen to them.
NET.9: I am the door. If anyone enters through me, he will be saved, and will come in and go out, and find pasture.
NET.10: The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy; I have come so that they may have life, and may have it abundantly.
NET.11: “I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
NET.12: The hired hand, who is not a shepherd and does not own sheep, sees the wolf coming and abandons the sheep and runs away. So the wolf attacks the sheep and scatters them.
NET.13: Because he is a hired hand and is not concerned about the sheep, he runs away.
NET.14: “I am the good shepherd. I know my own and my own know me —
NET.15: just as the Father knows me and I know the Father — and I lay down my life for the sheep.
NET.16: I have other sheep that do not come from this sheepfold. I must bring them too, and they will listen to my voice, so that there will be one flock and one shepherd.
NET.17: This is why the Father loves me — because I lay down my life, so that I may take it back again.
NET.18: No one takes it away from me, but I lay it down of my own free will. I have the authority to lay it down, and I have the authority to take it back again. This commandment I received from my Father.”
NET.19: Another sharp division took place among the Jewish people because of these words.
NET.20: Many of them were saying, “He is possessed by a demon and has lost his mind! Why do you listen to him?”
NET.21: Others said, “These are not the words of someone possessed by a demon. A demon cannot cause the blind to see, can it?”
NET.22: Then came the feast of the Dedication in Jerusalem.
NET.23: It was winter, and Jesus was walking in the temple area in Solomon’s Portico.
NET.24: The Jewish leaders surrounded him and asked, “How long will you keep us in suspense? If you are the Christ, tell us plainly.”
NET.25: Jesus replied, “I told you and you do not believe. The deeds I do in my Father’s name testify about me.
NET.26: But you refuse to believe because you are not my sheep.
NET.27: My sheep listen to my voice, and I know them, and they follow me.
NET.28: I give them eternal life, and they will never perish; no one will snatch them from my hand.
NET.29: My Father, who has given them to me, is greater than all, and no one can snatch them from my Father’s hand.
NET.30: The Father and I are one.”
NET.31: The Jewish leaders picked up rocks again to stone him to death.
NET.32: Jesus said to them, “I have shown you many good deeds from the Father. For which one of them are you going to stone me?”
NET.33: The Jewish leaders replied, “We are not going to stone you for a good deed but for blasphemy, because you, a man, are claiming to be God.”
NET.34: Jesus answered, “Is it not written in your law, ‘I said, you are gods’?
NET.35: If those people to whom the word of God came were called ‘gods’ (and the scripture cannot be broken),
NET.36: do you say about the one whom the Father set apart and sent into the world, ‘You are blaspheming,’ because I said, ‘I am the Son of God’?
NET.37: If I do not perform the deeds of my Father, do not believe me.
NET.38: But if I do them, even if you do not believe me, believe the deeds, so that you may come to know and understand that I am in the Father and the Father is in me.”
NET.39: Then they attempted again to seize him, but he escaped their clutches.
NET.40: Jesus went back across the Jordan River again to the place where John had been baptizing at an earlier time, and he stayed there.
NET.41: Many came to him and began to say, “John performed no miraculous sign, but everything John said about this man was true!”
NET.42: And many believed in Jesus there.
